commands.review_summary:
The PR removes Synology Chat webhook handler’s local 120s `Promise.race` around `deliver` and adds a regression test asserting authorized webhook delivery does not schedule that timeout.

PR surface: Source -4, Tests +31. Total +27 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main and `v2026.6.1` still schedule the 120000ms Synology Chat delivery timeout, and the PR body supplies before/after terminal output from a direct handler probe.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; the PR is already a narrow candidate for normal maintainer review and CI gating.

target.title: fix(synology-chat): remove local deliver timeout
target.body:
Fixes #59966.

## What changed

Removed the Synology Chat webhook handler's local `Promise.race` wrapper with the hardcoded 120s timeout. The handler now awaits `params.deliver(...)` directly, so the channel layer no longer overrides the core agent timeout configuration.

Added focused regression coverage proving a successful webhook delivery path no longer schedules a 120000ms timeout.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Synology Chat webhook delivery should not install a local 120s timeout wrapper around `deliver`; timeout policy should remain with the core agent runtime instead of being overridden by the channel handler.

**Real environment tested:** macOS arm64, Node v26.0.0, pnpm, OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/allahyar/Documents/fastino-tasks/openclaw-tui-59966`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** I exercised the Synology Chat webhook handler path from this branch with a direct `createWebhookHandler` probe that records the `setTimeout` delays scheduled during one authorized webhook request, then ran the focused Synology Chat webhook test shard:

```text
$ pnpm exec tsx --conditions=browser -e "<direct createWebhookHandler probe that records setTimeout delays during one authorized Synology webhook request>"
$ pnpm test extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts
$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error
$ git diff --check
```

**Before evidence optional:** The same direct handler probe on a clean `origin/main` checkout produced this terminal output:

```text
{
  "status": 204,
  "delays": [
    120000
  ],
  "hasHardcoded120sTimeout": true
}
```

**Evidence after fix:** The same direct handler probe on this branch produced this copied terminal output:

```text
{
  "status": 204,
  "delays": [],
  "hasHardcoded120sTimeout": false
}
```

The focused regression test also passed on this branch:

```text
$ pnpm test extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose
✓ |extension-messaging| extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts > createWebhookHandler > awaits deliver directly with no local hardcoded timeout wrapper 1ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  23 passed (23)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.02s
```

Format, lint, and whitespace checks passed:

```text
$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.ts extensions/synology-chat/src/webhook-handler.test.ts --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error
# passed with no output

$ git diff --check
# passed with no output
```

**Observed result after fix:** The authorized Synology Chat webhook path still returns HTTP 204 and calls through delivery, but the copied terminal output from the handler probe shows no local `120000`ms timeout is scheduled after this patch.

**Not tested:** Live Synology Chat network delivery against a real NAS instance.
target.author: sahibzada-allahyar
